description: An organism is 'lima' while it has life, whether it is a person, animal, plant, or fungus. The word settles the living condition but does not decide consciousness, health, or moral standing. 'lioru' (life) names the condition, while 'liona' (live) presents living as an activity or way of dwelling. A person who has died is 'rena to lumeo' (the one who died).
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l' and let air pass along its sides into stressed high-front unrounded 'i'. Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm', carry the voice through the nose, then open into central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The shared 'li' opening links 'lima' with 'liona' (live) and 'lioru' (life) inside Phi. Air leaves around the sides of the tongue and continues until the lips close for 'm', a smooth motion that helps the family stay in memory.

semantic_domains.nature: the living condition shared by organisms
semantic_domains.physical: the bodily distinction between being alive and having died
